proposed_hypotheses.text: Among prostate cancer patients, higher baseline ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Greater 6-month weight loss percentage (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by ECOG: ECOG 0 = 4.68 mo, ECOG 1 = 3.49 mo, ECOG 2 = 2.38 mo. OLS slope -1.161 mo per unit ECOG (t=-94.3, p<1e-300).
Pearson r=+0.123, p=1.5e-166. Each +1 g/dL albumin associated with longer PFS.
Pearson r=-0.143, p=1e-225. Greater weight loss associated with shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum PSA (psa_ng_ml, modeled on log scale) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher serum LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Patients with metastatic castration-resistant disease (mcrpc=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without (mcrpc=0).

analyses.result_summary: Log(PSA) vs PFS: Pearson r=-0.231, p<1e-300. Higher PSA strongly associated with shorter PFS.
LDH vs PFS: Pearson r=-0.017, p=1.8e-4. Statistically significant but small effect.
PFS mCRPC=1: 3.51 mo (n=27,481) vs mCRPC=0: 4.03 mo (n=22,519). Welch t-test p=1.6e-181, mean difference -0.521 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months (the conventional clinical expectation).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.856 (p<1e-300). Strikingly, older age is strongly associated with LONGER PFS, opposite to the conventional clinical expectation. PFS rises monotonically across age quintiles: 1.35 mo (<56.5y), 2.75 mo (56.5-62.4y), 3.72 mo (62.4-67.6y), 4.70 mo (67.6-73.5y), 6.24 mo (>73.5y). Effect direction is positive; original directional hypothesis is refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: in this cohort, older age_years is associated with LONGER pfs_months, with each additional year adding roughly +0.17 months of PFS in a univariate linear model.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: slope +0.174 mo per year of age (t≈373, p<1e-300). The age effect is the dominant univariate predictor of PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with enzalutamide (treatment_enzalutamide=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no enzalutamide.
Treatment with abiraterone (treatment_abiraterone=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no abiraterone.
Treatment with docetaxel (treatment_docetaxel=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no docetaxel.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS enza on=3.728 mo (n=20,076) vs off=3.755 mo (n=29,924); diff -0.026 mo, t-test p=0.153. No marginal effect.
Mean PFS abi on=3.740 mo (n=14,991) vs off=3.746 mo (n=35,009); diff -0.006 mo, p=0.776.
Mean PFS docetaxel on=3.732 mo (n=15,187) vs off=3.749 mo (n=34,813); diff -0.017 mo, p=0.381.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with olaparib (treatment_olaparib=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib in the overall cohort.
Treatment with lutetium-177 PSMA (treatment_lu177_psma=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no lu177-PSMA in the overall cohort.
Treatment with pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab in the overall cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS olaparib on=3.829 mo (n=5,098) vs off=3.735 mo (n=44,902); diff +0.094 mo, p=3.3e-3. Modest but significant positive effect overall.
Mean PFS Lu177 on=3.741 mo (n=7,504) vs off=3.745 mo (n=42,496); diff -0.004 mo, p=0.872. No marginal effect.
Mean PFS pembro on=3.805 mo (n=2,384) vs off=3.741 mo (n=47,616); diff +0.064 mo, p=0.137. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between olaparib (treatment_olaparib) and BRCA2 mutation (brca2_mutation): the olaparib benefit on pfs_months is larger in BRCA2-mutant patients than in BRCA2 wild-type patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation = +1.619 mo (p=4.3e-61). Main effect of olaparib in BRCA2- = -0.073 mo (p=0.020). PFS by cell: BRCA2-/olap- 3.74, BRCA2-/olap+ 3.67, BRCA2+/olap- 3.67, BRCA2+/olap+ 5.22. Strong synergistic benefit in BRCA2-mutant subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within BRCA2-mutant patients (brca2_mutation=1), olaparib treatment is associated with substantially longer pfs_months versus no olaparib (positive direction).
Within BRCA2 wild-type patients (brca2_mutation=0), olaparib treatment does NOT prolong pfs_months (effect estimate ~0 or slightly negative).

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ subgroup (n=4,996): olaparib on PFS=5.216 mo (n=529) vs off=3.671 mo (n=4,467); diff +1.545 mo, p=4.0e-27. Strongly supported.

BRCA2- subgroup (n=45,004): olaparib on PFS=3.668 mo (n=4,569) vs off=3.742 mo (n=40,435); diff -0.073 mo, p=0.019. Direction is slightly negative; consistent with no benefit and possibly small harm in BRCA2-WT.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab) and MSI-high status (msi_high): the pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in MSI-high patients than in MSI-stable patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.340 mo (p=0.153). PFS by cell: MSI-/pembro- 3.74, MSI-/pembro+ 3.81, MSI+/pembro- 3.79, MSI+/pembro+ 3.53 (n=79). Direction is opposite of hypothesized but underpowered; no statistical support for the canonical pembro-MSI interaction in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between Lu-177 PSMA therapy (treatment_lu177_psma) and PSMA-high status (psma_high): the Lu-177 PSMA benefit on pfs_months is larger in PSMA-high patients than in PSMA-low patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.017 mo (p=0.737). PFS in all four cells essentially identical (~3.73-3.75 mo). No PSMA-conditional benefit of Lu-177 detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a NEGATIVE interaction between enzalutamide (treatment_enzalutamide) and AR-V7 positivity (ar_v7_positive): AR-V7+ patients derive less PFS benefit (more negative effect) from enzalutamide than AR-V7- patients.
There is a NEGATIVE interaction between abiraterone (treatment_abiraterone) and AR-V7 positivity (ar_v7_positive) on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.0025 mo (p=0.957). All four cells within ~0.04 mo of each other. No detectable AR-V7-modifying effect of enzalutamide.
Interaction beta = +0.048 mo (p≈0.45). No detectable AR-V7-modifying effect of abiraterone.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with visceral metastases (visceral_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with liver metastases (liver_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with bone metastases (bone_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without.

analyses.result_summary: Visceral mets+ PFS=3.706 mo (n=9,973) vs visceral- PFS=3.754 mo; diff -0.048 mo, p=0.034. Small but significant.
Liver mets+ PFS=3.743 vs 3.744; diff -0.002 mo, p=0.948. No effect.
Bone mets+ PFS=3.753 vs 3.742; diff +0.011 mo, p=0.624. No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, albumin, log(PSA), weight loss, LDH, and visceral mets in a multivariable OLS, mCRPC will lose its univariate negative association with pfs_months (i.e., mCRPC's apparent prognostic effect is confounded by other prognostic factors).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + np.log(psa_ng_ml) + ldh_u_l + visceral_mets', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In adjusted model, mcrpc beta = -0.0017 mo (p=0.657, NS). Adjusted prognostic effects: age +0.174 (p<1e-300), ecog -1.150 (p<1e-300), albumin +0.478 (p<1e-300), weight_loss -0.077 (p<1e-300), log_psa -0.349 (p<1e-300), ldh -0.0005 (p<1e-300), visceral -0.017 (p=3e-5). mCRPC's univariate effect is fully confounded by these covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The olaparib × BRCA2 interaction effect on pfs_months remains large and statistically significant after adjusting for age, ECOG, albumin, weight loss, and log(PSA).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation + age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + np.log(psa_ng_ml)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction (olap×BRCA2) = +1.435 mo (p≈0). Main effects of olap and brca2 each ~0 (p≥0.68). Interaction is robust to confounder adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race/ethnicity (race_ethnicity) is associated with pfs_months in this cohort.
Insurance type (insurance_type) is associated with pfs_months.
Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
Rural residence (rural_residence=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than urban residence.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by race: white 3.741, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, asian 3.797, other 3.749. ANOVA F=0.564, p=0.689. No association.
Mean PFS by insurance: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA F=0.344, p=0.794. No association.
Pearson r=-0.0037, p=0.402. No association.
Rural PFS=3.756 vs non-rural=3.741; diff +0.015 mo, p=0.504. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race/ethnicity disparities in pfs_months emerge after adjusting for age, ECOG, albumin, and log(PSA).
Insurance type disparities in pfs_months emerge after adjusting for age, ECOG, albumin, and log(PSA).

analyses.result_summary: All race coefficients (vs asian baseline) within |0.02 mo|, p>=0.24. All insurance coefficients within |0.007 mo|, p>=0.57. No demographic disparity in PFS detected even after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Symptom burden — fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade — is each negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All five symptom-grade vs PFS correlations have |r|<0.003 and p>0.65 (fatigue r=+0.002 p=0.66; pain r=-0.001 p=0.80; dyspnea r=-0.001 p=0.81; cough r=+0.001 p=0.79; appetite_loss r=-0.0005 p=0.91). Symptom grades are not associated with PFS in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Common comorbidities — diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure — are each negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All 5 comorbidities show |diff| < 0.04 mo and p>0.18: DM diff=-0.029 p=0.19; HTN -0.024 p=0.18; COPD +0.019 p=0.36; CKD +0.033 p=0.23; HF -0.003 p=0.92. None individually associated with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Common somatic alterations beyond BRCA2 — tp53_mutation, pten_loss, cdkn2a_loss, pik3ca_mutation, her2_amplification, keap1_mutation, fgfr_alteration — are each associated (any direction) with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All seven mutations: |diff| < 0.05 mo and p>=0.10. tp53 diff=+0.003 p=0.87; pten -loss +0.021 p=0.62; cdkn2a-loss -0.042 p=0.10; pik3ca -0.014 p=0.66; her2-amp +0.020 p=0.70; keap1 -0.022 p=0.36; fgfr -0.039 p=0.53. None significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 25 single-nucleotide polymorphisms (snp_*) shows an association (any direction) with pfs_months passing a Bonferroni threshold of 0.05/25=0.002.

analyses.code: for s in snp_cols: ANOVA or pearsonr; sort by p
analyses.result_summary: Smallest SNP p-values: snp_rs4986893 p=0.014; snp_rs429358 p=0.064; snp_rs1065852 p=0.089; snp_rs1799983 p=0.118; snp_rs1800896 p=0.224. None below the Bonferroni threshold (0.002). No SNP-PFS signal in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Prior treatment history — prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy, and prior_lines_of_therapy — predicts shorter pfs_months (heavier-treated patients have worse outcomes).

analyses.code: Welch t-tests on each prior-treatment binary; pearsonr for prior_lines_of_therapy
analyses.result_summary: All five binary prior-treatment indicators give |diff|≤0.030 mo and p≥0.12 (prior_chemotherapy diff=-0.010 p=0.59; prior_radiation +0.007 p=0.71; prior_surgery -0.030 p=0.12; prior_immunotherapy +0.022 p=0.39; prior_targeted_therapy +0.021 p=0.46). prior_lines_of_therapy r=-0.006, p=0.21. No prior-treatment effect detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a NEGATIVE interaction between age_years and mcrpc on pfs_months: among mCRPC patients the per-year age advantage is smaller than among non-mCRPC patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.0047 mo per year (p=3.3e-7). Each additional year of age adds +0.177 mo of PFS in non-mCRPC but only +0.172 mo in mCRPC. Statistically significant but small in magnitude; signal is mostly carried by age main effect (+0.177 mo/yr).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable OLS that includes all six treatments, prognostic factors (age, ECOG, albumin, weight loss, log(PSA), LDH, visceral mets, mCRPC), BRCA2 status, and the olaparib×BRCA2 interaction, only the olaparib×BRCA2 interaction term among treatment-related parameters is associated with pfs_months at p<0.001.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + np.log(psa_ng_ml) + ldh_u_l + visceral_mets + mcrpc + treatment_enzalutamide + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_docetaxel + treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_lu177_psma', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients: enzalutamide -0.006 (p=0.05), abiraterone -0.001 (p=0.67), docetaxel -0.003 (p=0.28), olaparib +0.0001 (p=0.98), pembrolizumab +0.012 (p=0.07), lu177 -0.006 (p=0.13), brca2 -0.001 (p=0.92), olaparib×BRCA2 +1.435 (p<1e-300). Confirmed: only the olaparib×BRCA2 interaction is a meaningful treatment-related driver of PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The olaparib×BRCA2 interaction effect on pfs_months is similar in magnitude in the mCRPC subset and the non-mCRPC subset.
Within BRCA2-mutant patients, olaparib's PFS benefit remains large after adjusting for age, ECOG, albumin, and log(PSA).

analyses.code: for mc in [0,1]: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation', data=df[df['mcrpc']==mc]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction olap×BRCA2: in mCRPC=0 subset beta=+1.627 (p=1.3e-28); in mCRPC=1 subset beta=+1.630 (p=6.0e-36). Effects nearly identical — the BRCA2-mutant olaparib benefit is independent of castration-resistance status.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib + age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + np.log(psa_ng_ml)', data=df[df['brca2_mutation']==1]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ subgroup adjusted model: olaparib coefficient +1.446 mo (t=38.0, p<1e-300), with age +0.172, ecog -1.147, albumin +0.457, log_psa -0.358. Olaparib's benefit in BRCA2-mutant patients is robust to confounder adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sex_female is a constant (=0) in this prostate cancer cohort; thus sex cannot show an association with pfs_months.

A simple parsimonious model with five prognostic variables (age_years, ecog_ps, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, log(psa_ng_ml)) plus the olaparib×BRCA2 interaction explains a large fraction of pfs_months variation, and adds substantively over an age-only baseline.

analyses.result_summary: sex_female mean = 0.0 (all 50,000 records are male). Variable is degenerate; no association with PFS can be tested. Confirms cohort is purely male, consistent with prostate cancer.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years', data=df).fit().rsquared; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + np.log(psa_ng_ml) + treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation', data=df).fit().rsquared
analyses.result_summary: Age-only R^2 = 0.732. Parsimonious 5-prognostic + olap×BRCA2 model R^2 = 0.974. Adding ECOG, albumin, weight loss, log(PSA), and the olaparib×BRCA2 interaction lifts explained variance by +0.242. The dominant signal is age, with ECOG and the BRCA2-targeted PARP inhibitor benefit as the next-largest contributors.
